Description.Name: The Other Signal
Description.Details: Almost three months have passed. No contact from Joanna. No contact from anyone affiliated with the Minnesota Tribe. Farah stripped out the hardware personally and ran three diagnostic sweeps afterward. Whatever Joanna could see through the Argo ship is dark now. Sumire has been monitoring every frequency the Tribe was known to use. Old [[DM.BaseDescriptionDefs[LoreDeepPeriphery],Deep Periphery]] signal bands. Watch intercept cross-references. Nothing that isn't background static. Then a signal arrives on the tertiary monitoring array, coming through a channel frequency that the removed Sigma Ghost Array was never routing. A channel not in its interception table. Darius reads the authentication header twice before saying anything. "Commander. You need to see this." He calls the senior crew to the briefing room without explanation, just the priority code that means drop everything and come now. Yang arrives first, still wiping grease off his hands from the mech bay. Farah follows moments later, medical datapad still in hand. Sumire is already at her station, the signal trace running across her console. Darius brings up the message on the main display without preamble. "This came in on a channel the surveillance hardware was never monitoring. Clean signal. Old [[DM.Factions[faction_SLDF],SLDF]] encryption protocols. Read it." INCOMING ENCRYPTED BURST AUTHENTICATION: SLDF CRYPTOGRAPHIC STANDARD, SERIES 7 — CHAIN OF CUSTODY VERIFIED CHANNEL: TERTIARY MONITORING BAND 4 — UNMONITORED "Commander {COMMANDER.Callsign}. I will keep this brief. My name is Marcus Hammerick. I hold the rank of Star Captain within the [[DM.Factions[faction_MinnesotaTribe],Minnesota Tribe]], not the faction you have been working with, but what the Tribe should have remained. I know what happened on St. Andreas. I know what Joanna has had you doing. I know about the equipment installed on your ship, because my people warned against placing it there and lost that argument. I am asking for one conversation. Just words. If what I say does not ring true, just leave, and I will not contact you again. But I believe you deserve to understand what you have been part of before you decide what comes next. — Star Captain Hammerick" Darius sets the datapad down. "He knows about the tap hardware." Sumire is already running a trace, shaking her head. "Signal source is masked. Old SLDF cryptographic routing. He's careful." Yang crosses his arms. "Another Tribe operative wanting something from us. Wonderful." Farah reviews the authentication chain. "Series Seven SLDF encryption. Pre-Exodus codes. You cannot generate these from scratch, you inherit them. If he has these, he is legitimate Minnesota Tribe. Not Watch fabrication." Darius leans on the console. "Could still be a trap. But we have nothing else. No leads on Joanna, no idea what she is building, no contacts. This is the only clean signal we have gotten in two weeks." Yang grunts. "If he is lying, we will know soon enough. And we can always walk away after." The crew waits for your decision. A mysterious contact claiming to represent the real Minnesota Tribe. Someone who knows about the surveillance hardware. Someone offering answers. It could be a trap. Or it could be the only chance to understand what you've been part of.

Description.Name: Accept his request for a conversation
Accept his request for a conversation
Description.Details: <color=#BFBFBF>You head into the conference room and open a return channel on the same band. The response comes back immediately, as if he has been waiting.</color>
